---
title: "Communications"
---

::: {.page-lead}
Talks, awards, media coverage, and the mentoring and training that have shaped my work.
:::

[Awards]{.eyebrow .lg}

- **Best Oral Presentation Award**, DUNES Symposium, Singapore, 2025
- **Best Poster Award**, SfN Singapore Chapter Annual Meeting, Singapore, 2024
- **2nd Place Poster Award**, Northwest Developmental Biology Conference, Seattle, 2018

I've presented my research at international meetings including NeuroFly 2024 (Birmingham, UK), the
2024 Asia-Pacific Drosophila Neurobiology Conference (Tokyo, Japan), and the 2025 Francis Crick
Symposium (Suzhou, China).

[]{.section-rule}

[Courses & training]{.eyebrow .lg}

:::: {.stack}

::: {.scard}
[Chen Institute–Duke–NUS AI Boot Camp](https://www.cheninstitute.org/events-new/duke-bootcamp){.scard-title}
[Duke-NUS · June 2026]{.scard-meta}

I attended a selective four-day AI Bootcamp run by Duke-NUS and Duke University's Department of
Computer Science to build a stronger foundation in modern AI, from how large language and foundation
models work to designing and evaluating AI workflows, along with their limitations and responsible
use. My goal was to build a solid understanding of these tools before applying them to
high-throughput behavioral data.
:::

::::

[]{.section-rule}

[Media]{.eyebrow .lg}

:::: {.stack}

::: {.scard}
[Duke-NUS researchers develop light-controlled "switch" for brain cells to better study the brain](https://www.straitstimes.com/singapore/duke-nus-researchers-develop-light-controlled-switch-for-brain-cells-to-better-study-the-brain){.scard-title}
[The Straits Times · 2024]{.scard-meta}

Media coverage of our *Nature Communications* paper introducing kalium channelrhodopsins (KCRs), a
light-controlled neuronal "off switch" with greater selectivity than existing optogenetic silencers,
for which I led the *C. elegans* behavioral work validating the approach across species.

[Read the article →](https://www.straitstimes.com/singapore/duke-nus-researchers-develop-light-controlled-switch-for-brain-cells-to-better-study-the-brain){.read-more}
:::

::: {.scard}
[DUNES Symposium 2025](https://www.duke-nus.edu.sg/newshub/our-stories/dunes-symposium-2025){.scard-title}
[Duke-NUS Newshub · 2025]{.scard-meta}

Duke-NUS featured my work following the 17th DUNES Symposium, where my presentation on optogenetic
control of valence and locomotion in *Drosophila* received the Best Oral Presentation award.

[Read the feature →](https://www.duke-nus.edu.sg/newshub/our-stories/dunes-symposium-2025){.read-more}
:::

::::

[]{.section-rule}

[Mentoring]{.eyebrow .lg}

I've mentored three undergraduate researchers. In 2024 at Duke-NUS, I supervised a Yale-NUS College
undergraduate on the OPN3 optogenetic silencing project, helping with experimental design, data
collection, and analysis. Their work contributed to the study, and they later joined the lab as a
full-time research assistant. Earlier, from 2016 to 2018, I mentored two undergraduate researchers
in the Cabernard lab on their own research projects.
